response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with 15+ years of experience, but the core requirement for this role is modern C++ expertise applied to industrial automation, and the resume shows only a brief ~6-month C/C++ stint at National Instruments back in 2014–2015. The bulk of recent work is in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms, with no evidence of multi-threaded C++ systems, message brokers, MQTT/industrial protocols, or IEC 62443 / SDL practice. Without recent, substantive C++ and industrial-cybersecurity exposure, the fit for a Senior Manager — Digital Solutions (C++) role is weak.
strengths: 15+ years overall engineering experience with architecture and mentoring exposure
Strong testing/TDD, CI/CD, and software craftsmanship background
concerns: Only ~6 months of C/C++ experience, and it was over a decade ago
No demonstrated industrial automation, MQTT, Modbus/OPC UA, or IEC 62443 / SDL experience
Recent stack is JS/TS/Node-focused, not aligned with the C++/STL/OOP core requirement
